title: Gently Spiced Ethiopian Lamb Fir Fir
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish made with lamb, red pepper, and herbal spices, served in a clay tajine, this recipe is a romantic and light take on the classic fir fir, with a kosher-friendly twist using lardons-render for added depth of flavor.
instructions: Heat the rendered lardons in a clay tajine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the red pepper flakes, berbere spice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 1 cup of water to the tajine and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours.
Stir in the torn injera bread and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Serve the fir fir hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
